task: Build pairs (x,y) where x != y; the else/continue is redundant
error_location: lines 6-7: else: continue is a no-op (loop continues anyway)
clarification_query: Is the else: continue block necessary when it's the last statement in the if/else?
clarification_options: no, continue is the default loop behavior
yes, it makes the intent explicit
yes, without it x==y pairs would be included
no, but it prevents the append
expected_user_response: no, continue is the default loop behavior
corrected_code: result = [(x,y) for x in range(5) for y in range(5) if x != y]
explanation: After an if block, the loop continues to the next iteration automatically. else: continue adds no effect and can be removed.
